items.summary:

expected to turn the <code>SELinuxMount</code> <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/command-line-tools-reference/feature-gates/">feature gate</a> on by
items.reason: Introduces upcoming SELinux feature gate changes in Kubernetes v1.37 – a significant security‑related update that should be documented in the Wiki.

items.title: Trivy, KICS, and the shape of supply chain attacks so far in 2026
items.summary: Catching the KICS push: what happened, and the case for open, fast collaboration In the past few weeks we've worked through two supply chain compromises on Docker Hub with a similar shape: first Trivy, now Checkmarx KICS. In both cases, stolen publisher credentials were used to push malicious images
items.reason: Analysis of recent supply‑chain attacks involving Trivy and KICS; relevant for security and DevSecOps documentation.

items.title: Why MicroVMs: The Architecture Behind Docker Sandboxes
items.summary: Last week, we launched Docker Sandboxes with a bold goal: to deliver the strongest agent isolation in the market. This post unpacks that claim, how microVMs enable it, and some of the architectural choices we made in this approach. The Problem With Every Other Approach Every sandboxing model asks yo
items.reason: Explanation of Docker Sandboxes built on MicroVMs – important for container isolation and runtime architecture topics.

items.title: Why We Chose the Harder Path: Docker Hardened Images, One Year Later
items.summary: We're coming up on a year since launching Docker Hardened Images (DHI) last May, and crossing a milestone earlier this month made me stop and reflect on what we've actually been building. Earlier this month, we crossed over 500k daily pulls of DHIs, and over 25k continuously patched OS level artifac
items.reason: One‑year retrospective on Docker Hardened Images; useful for a Docker security‑hardening guide.

items.reason: Guide on analyzing Hugging Face projects for Arm64 readiness using Docker tools – relevant for ARM ecosystem documentation.

items.title: oven-sh/bun bun-v1.3.13
items.summary:
To install Bun v1.3.13
```bash
curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash
# or you can use npm
# npm install -g bun
```

Windows:
```bash
powershell -c "irm bun.sh/install.ps1|iex"
```

To upgrade to Bun v1.3.13:
```bash
bun upgrade
```

### [Read Bun v1.3.13's release notes on Bun's
items.reason: Bun v1.3.13 release – important for a JavaScript runtime version catalog.
